endpoints.name: Galarian Darumaka (Darkness Ablaze 043/189; 2nd print, species duplicate of RBC-047)

description: Wave 22 extends the galarian-regional-forms cohort with two new species (Galarian Mr. Mime DAA-035 + Galarian Mr. Rime DAA-036) — the cohort's first complete in-corpus Galarian-evolution-line pair (base form + its Galar-exclusive SwSh-introduced evolved endpoint, mirroring the Galarian Farfetch'd → Sirfetch'd pattern). A second Galarian Darumaka print (DAA-043) also lands; pointer added to its frontmatter but not added to node appears_on (species already represented by RBC-047). The Mr. Mime ↔ Mr. Rime pair gets a 1:1 evolution-mirror Connections section in both card MDs, framing the cohort's clearest case of Galar's UK-modeled cultural register (British music-hall + tap-dance + Charlie-Chaplin citation) reshaping a species' canonical identity. Combined stack-plus-mirror in one slot — the mirror is the Galar-exclusive-evolution pattern's clearest in-corpus instance and earns its own edge.
citations: [Bulbapedia: Mr. Mime (Galarian Form), Mr. Rime, Galarian Darumaka per-species pages]
notes: Two endpoints. Both equal nodes. Must be traversable from either side. Hence the Mr. Mime ↔ Mr. Rime mirror. The sigh is professional, not personal. The stack-plus-mirror combine was clearly the right call — drawing a card↔card mirror for the Galar-exclusive evolution and ALSO extending the cohort node in one pass is the structurally efficient move, and the two edges don't overlap in coverage (cohort fan vs species-pair mirror). Galar-exclusive evolution lines are a designer-coordinated structural feature worth its own edge separate from cohort membership.
